meta.title: Does supervision matter? A randomized controlled study of laparoscopic simulator training in undergraduate education
meta.notes: Table 2 in the PDF appears garbled/misaligned in transcription (rows for within-group deltas split oddly); between-group p-values and means read from the clean cells. Some prose/table inconsistencies noted (e.g. shooter SD values, mobile p 0.101 vs 0.102, feedback p 0.684 vs 0.685 in text).

claims.text: The supervised group showed significant improvement in procedure time from warm-up to first exercise (388s vs 168s; p<0.001).

location.quote: the Supervised group (388s vs 168s; p < 0.001)

claims.predicate: paired_test_p_consistent_with_means; reported_p < 0.001 for warm-up (388) vs Test 1 (168) in supervised group
claims.scope: Supervised group (n=8), paired warm-up vs Test 1

claims.text: The independent group showed significant improvement in procedure time from warm-up to first exercise (347s vs 213s; p=0.020).

location.quote: the independent group (347s vs 213s; p = 0.020)

claims.predicate: paired_test_p_consistent_with_means; reported_p == 0.020 for warm-up (347) vs Test 1 (213) in independent group
claims.scope: Independent group (n=12), paired warm-up vs Test 1

claims.text: No statistically significant difference between groups in the primary outcome (Test 1 completion time), p=0.108.

location.quote:
did not reach
statistical significance ( p = 0.108)

claims.predicate: between_group_p == 0.108 for Test 1 supervised (168±47) vs independent (213±72)

claims.text: Supervised group completed Test 1 in mean 168 s (SD 47) vs 213 s (SD 72) in independent group; mean difference -45 s, 95% CI -108 to +18 s.

location.quote:
The supervised group
completed Test 1 in a mean of 168 s (SD 47), compared with
213 s (SD 72) in the independent group. The mean difference
was −45 s, with a 95% CI ranging from −108 to +18 s

claims.text: Procedure times for first and second exercises tended to be slightly shorter in supervised group (p=0.108 and p=0.124).

location.quote:
slightly shorter
in the supervised group compared with the independent group
( p = 0.108 and p = 0.124, respectively)

claims.predicate: between_group_p Test1 == 0.108, Test2 == 0.124
claims.scope: Test 1 and Test 2, supervised vs independent

claims.text: Supervised group reported significantly lower perceived difficulty after training (4.1 vs 3.5; p=0.049).

location.quote:
reported significantly lower perceived difficulty after training
compared to pre-training (4.1 vs 3.5; p = 0.049)

claims.predicate: paired_test_p == 0.049 for perceived difficulty before (4.1) vs after (3.5) in supervised group

claims.text: Independent group's feedback value score significantly reduced to half baseline (4.6 vs 2.6; p=0.003).

location.quote:
post-training score was significantly reduced to half of the
baseline value (4.6 vs 2.6; p = 0.003)

claims.predicate: paired_test_p == 0.003 for feedback value before (4.6) vs after (2.6) in independent group; 2.6 ~= half of 4.6

claims.text: In supervised group, feedback value pre/post scores did not change significantly (4.5 vs 4.6; p=0.685).

location.quote:
remaining at the
same level (4.5 vs 4.6; p = 0.685)

claims.predicate: paired_test_p == 0.685 (or 0.684 per table) for feedback value before (4.5) vs after (4.6) in supervised group

completed examination test 3 in a mean of

claims.predicate: between_group_p == 0.063 for test 3 gaming (137) vs no gaming (249)
claims.scope: Test 3, gaming vs no gaming experience

completed test 3 significantly faster than those who did not

claims.predicate: between_group_p == 0.037 for test 3 shooter (130±13) vs non-shooter (207±130)

a trend toward faster

claims.predicate: between_group_p ~ 0.102 (table reports 0.101) for test 3 mobile vs non-mobile

claims.text: Trend for men to complete test 3 slightly faster than women (140±33 s vs 208±134 s; p=0.092).

location.quote:
a trend for men to complete live test 3 slightly faster

claims.predicate: between_group_p == 0.092 for test 3 males (140±33) vs females (208±134)

claims.text: No significant sex differences in Warm-up (p=0.627), Test 1 (p=0.639), Test 2 (p=0.233).

location.quote:
No significant
differences were found in Warm-up (p = 0.627), Test 1 (p = 0.639),

claims.predicate: between_group_p Warmup==0.627, Test1==0.639, Test2==0.233 for sex differences

claims.text: In paired warm-up vs test 1, women improved significantly (173 s; p=0.001) whereas men less (159 s; p=0.058).

location.quote:
women
improved their times significantly (173 s; p = 0.001) whereas men

claims.predicate: paired_test_p women==0.001 (improvement 173s), men==0.058 (improvement 159s)

claims.text: A priori power calculation indicated 36 participants required for 70% power; only 20 enrolled.

location.quote:
a total of 
36 trainees would be required to achieve 70% power to detect a

claims.predicate: sample_size_calc: required N == 36 for 70% power; enrolled N == 20

claims.text: Post-hoc achieved power ranged approximately 28% to 35%.

location.quote:
the achieved power was 
approximately 28%–35%

claims.text: Prior study basis: guided group improved rope performance by 2.1 mm/s vs control 0.6 mm/s, SD 1.8 mm/s.

improved rope test

claims.text: Conclusion: remote real-time supervision significantly reduces perceived difficulty and prevents devaluation of feedback compared to independent practice.

Remote real-time supervision significantly reduces perceived

practice.
